```python
import pint
import pandas as pd
ureg = pint.UnitRegistry()
```

issue_title: Enable use of different files depending on build environment
issue_body:
We're currently trying to get a Xilinx DRAM controller (MIG) to work. In the core file, we include the IP-XACT file (*.xci), which then causes Vivado to generate the code for this IP. Right now, it [looks like that](https://github.com/wallento/fusesoc_cores/blob/master/boards/nexys4ddr/nexys4ddr-ddr.core):

```
[fileset core]
usage = vivado
files =
  ip/mig_7series.xci[file_type=xci]
  ip/mig_config.prj[file_type=data]
```

Unfortunately, (almost) each Vivado version comes with a new version of the MIG core. Xilinx provides a  `upgrade_ip` TCL command to upgrade between the different cores. That works well as long as the core API does not change. So using a old version of the core and then upgrading it might work for many use cases.

When the core API changes, this does not work. In these cases, we need to include different source files depending on the used Vivado version: a different xci, prj and verilog file which wraps the core.

What would be the right way to support such a feature? I propose to have "conditional filesets", e.g. like this:

```
[fileset core_mig_3.x]
usage = vivado
onlyif = $vivado_version == 2016.2 || $vivado_version == 2016.1
files =
  rtl/verilog/mig_wrapper.v
  ip/mig_7series.xci[file_type=xci]
  ip/mig_config.prj[file_type=data]


[fileset core_mig_2.x]
usage = vivado
onlyif = $vivado_version == 2015.1 or $vivado_version == 2015.2
files =
  rtl/verilog/mig_wrapper.v
  ip/mig_7series.xci[file_type=xci]
  ip/mig_config.prj[file_type=data]
```

The `onlyif` would be a new command which evaluates an arbitrary conditional expression. As variables we provide a set of "facts" about the system, which can be provided by different "facts" classes. The `$vivado_version` is just one example. This idea is taken from Puppet/facter, where this concept works really well.

@olofk , @wallento: what do you think?

issue_title: add "clear" to MetricsMock
issue_body:
If you want to use `MetricsMock` with pytest and fixtures, you might make a fixture like this:

```
from markus.testing import MetricsMock

@pytest.fixture
def metricsmock():
    with MetricsMock() as mm:
        yield mm


def test_something(metricsmock):
    # do things that create metrics
    assert metricsmock.has_record('incr', stat='something', value=1)
```

The thing `MetricsMock` is missing is the ability to clear the records.

This issue covers implementing that.

issue_title: Connection receive_timeout regression when updating from 2.2.4 to 2.4
issue_body:
We updated from ldap3 2.2.4 to ldap3 2.4 and one of our integration tests caught a regression around the `receive_timeout` argument of `Connection` objects.

In our application, we create a `Connection` object with a `receive_timeout` argument of 7 seconds. Later we call `conn.start_tls()`. In our integration test, this expected to error our not earlier than after 7 seconds. With ldap3 2.2.4 this test passes.

After updating to ldap3 2.4 our integration test fails: the timeout error is actually raised after roughly 1 second.

I saw https://github.com/cannatag/ldap3/issues/356 and did a bit of debugging with the following patch to `strategies/sync.py`:

```
$ diff -wu sync.py.orig sync.py
--- sync.py.orig	2017-11-23 18:16:40.536759062 +0100
+++ sync.py	2017-11-23 18:16:14.274747513 +0100
@@ -80,8 +80,18 @@
         while receiving:
             if get_more_data:
                 try:
+
+                    import socket
+                    print('socket option SO_RCVTIMEO:')
+                    print(self.connection.socket.getsockopt(socket.SOL_SOCKET, socket.SO_RCVTIMEO))
+                    print('socket.gettimeout:')
+                    print(self.connection.socket.gettimeout())
+
                     data = self.connection.socket.recv(self.socket_size)
                 except (OSError, socket.error, AttributeError) as e:
+                    print(str(e))
+                    print(type(e))
+
                     self.connection.last_error = 'error receiving data: ' + str(e)
                     exc = e
```
This prints
```
socket option SO_RCVTIMEO:
7
socket.gettimeout:
1.0
timed out
<class 'socket.timeout'>
```
That is, the `SO_RCVTIMEO` option has been set as expected. However, `socket.gettimeout()` is the method corresponding to `socket.settimeout()`, and it returns 1 instead of 7. `socket.settimeout()` seems to be the only "official way" to actually set a timeout value from CPython's `socket` module. One important aspect to check next is whether or not ldap3 2.4 actually uses the `socket.settimeout()` method for setting the timeout.

Note that the `Server`'s `connect_timeout` is set to 1 s. 

This has been observed with CPython 3.6.3.

issue_title: Incorrect indentation in generated file
issue_body:
This is all tested with the graphviz python package in version `0.7.1`, which is currently the latest.

Here is a test script

```py
#!/usr/bin/env python2

import graphviz

graph = graphviz.Graph()
graph.edge("a", "b")
subgraph = graphviz.Graph(body=["rank=same"])
subgraph.node("sub", label="hero")
subgraph.edge("sub", "zero")
graph.subgraph(subgraph)
graph.edge("a", "sub")
graph.edge("b", "zero")

print graph
```

which generates the following output

```
graph {
                a -- b
        {
        rank=same
                sub [label=hero]
                        sub -- zero
        }
                a -- sub
                b -- zero
}
```

We can see that some lines are incorrectly indented: in this case the edge lines and the rank line.

What we expect in this case is the following

```
graph {
        a -- b
        {
                rank=same
                sub [label=hero]
                sub -- zero
        }
        a -- sub
        b -- zero
}
```

Here is the graph, as rendered by dot, for completeness:

![graphvizbadindent](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/3617724/26636941/6cc31e50-461e-11e7-8db0-57705d428843.png)

issue_title: error with @autoclass concerning default arguments' type
issue_body:
I would like to provide an example showing how pytypes can be used with autoclass.
However the following code fails with a strange error:

```python
from autoclass import autoargs, autoprops, Boolean
from pytypes import typechecked
from numbers import Real, Integral
from typing import Optional

@typechecked
@autoprops
class HouseConfiguration(object):
    @autoargs
    def __init__(self,
                 name: str,
                 surface: Real,
                 nb_floors: Optional[Integral] = 1,
                 with_windows: Boolean = False):
        pass

    # -- overriden setter for surface for custom validation
    @setter_override
    def surface(self, surface):
        assert surface > 0
        self._surface = surface

t = HouseConfiguration('test', 12, 2)  # error
```

The error received is : 

```bash
Expected: Tuple[str, Real, Union[Integral, NoneType], Boolean]
Received: Tuple[str, int, int, int]
```

While this works:
```python
t = HouseConfiguration('test', 12, nb_floors=2)
```

So it seems that this does not have anything to do with autoclass: somehow positional arguments / default values in the constructor signature are not handled properly.

issue_title: Cisco IOS Command "ip host $hostname $ipaddress" is not properly detected as config mode command
issue_body:
The command used in Cisco IOS for setting a hostname within the configuration, example:
```
ip host tftp 192.168.1.1
```

issue_body:
Test command string is: "rd-set 1111:22222\n"
netmike method used is: 
received_data = \
              ssh_handle.send_command_expect( cmd_data.split( '\n' )[0],
                                              delay_factor=delaycnt,
                                              max_loops=240,
                                              strip_prompt=True,
                                              strip_command=True )
Problem:  The system will hang when the above method is called.
None reason for problem:  The cisco command rd-set is a sub-system.  Unfortunately and unlike other commands subsystems where its possible to string the entire subsystem command into one string the issuing of the following command string will not work:  "rd-set 1111:222222 end-set\n", the system will complain.  It appears the XRv requires separated input streams.  While using a "cut/paste" into the console will work, building a stream such as "rd-set 1111:22222 \n end-set \n" (note the linefeeds) will not work.
Any suggestions?
Easy way to observe the problem is to use a typical "show running-config" output file which has the indented format as a pushed file through the netmiko method "send_command_expect"

thanks...Chris

issue_title: SSHTunnelForwarder.daemon_forward_servers is not respected
issue_body:
Opening this issue to get any thoughts before I do a PR.

Test case:

In ipython, construct a tunnel using:
```
tun = SSHTunnelForwarder((ssh_host, 22), ssh_username=user,
                              remote_bind_address=remote, local_bind_address=('127.0.0.1',),
                              ssh_pkey=ssh_key)
tun.daemon_forward_servers = True
tun.daemon_transport = True
tun.start()

In [12]: [(repr(t), t.isDaemon()) for t in threading.enumerate()]
Out[12]:
[('<_MainThread(MainThread, started 140736862168000)>', False),
 ('<HistorySavingThread(IPythonHistorySavingThread, started 123145316188160)>',
  True),
 ('<paramiko.Transport at 0xf68c048 (cipher aes128-ctr, 128 bits) (active; 1 open channel(s))>',
  True),
 ('<Thread(Srv-61825, started daemon 123145326698496)>', True),
 ('<Thread(Thread-282, started daemon 123145337208832)>', False)]
```

That last one should also be a daemon. The workaround is to set `_ThreadingForwardServer.daemon_threads=True`.

Questions:

* Is there a particular reason to have a global `DAEMON`?
  * It looks like public interface, but you can't update it once classes are initialized.
  * And, yuck, globals.
* Is there any benefit to having a separate `daemon_forward_servers` and `daemon_transport` field?

Proposal:
1. replace `DAEMON` with literal `False`
  * Still want a default to protect from crashes.
2. `_make_ssh_forward_server` sets `ssh_forward_server.daemon_threads = self.daemon_forward_servers`
3. Replace `SSHTunnelForwarder.daemon_*` with `.daemon`.

I traced the reason is due to the commit:
commit dd4069b5f4b5dfa0417c4b1ccf291f558133fb95
Author: Peter V. Saveliev <petr.savelyev@ericsson.com>
Date:   Thu Nov 26 12:43:08 2015 +0100

issue_title: Consider calling utility functions in Mappable
Doc builders build from different directories
issue_body:
This issue lies more with the larger ecosystem than with rst.linker, but it affects this project.

Since `rst.linker` takes as input the filenames to be linked, those filenames must be relative to something, either the project root or the cwd. But since these two things are often not the same due to rtfd/readthedocs.org#890 and the fact that `setup.py build_sphinx` will typically build from the project root, there's no reliable relative directory from which to reference files.

As a result, rst.linker will fail with an error like 

```
  File "/var/folders/c6/v7hnmq453xb6p2dbz1gqc6rr0000gn/T/rwt-6_7k_t6k/rst/linker.py", line 162, in write_links
    with io.open(source, encoding='utf-8') as source:
FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: '../CHANGES.rst'
```

issue_title: 'from' can't be used as a keyword argument in the email notifier.
issue_body:

Maybe 'from' should be renamed to avoid the problem.  A work-around is calling notify with dictionary unpacking. But that should probably be documented.


`>>> from notifiers import get_notfier`

`SyntaxError: invalid syntax`

`>>> p.notify(**{'to':'someone@example.com', 'message':'Will work', 'from':'sender@example.com'})`

issue_body:
The module works very well for calculating the exact date/time. But there is a specific use case that I'm working towards, would like to understand how to exactly implement it.

**1. Date Ranges**
25 - 31 Dec
_The output here would be 31st Dec, but I'd like to construct a date range. A possible way could be use regular expressions to convert it to 25 Dec - 31 Dec. But I want to understand if there is some part of the module that'll help me achieve this functionality out of the box._

**2. Time Ranges**
3 - 4 pm
_Same as above_

**3. Overriding default definitions**
So pdt has certain definitions for words like morning, noon, evening. I was hoping to override those values with the ones that suit me. Would like to know how to go about that.

**4. Adding more definitions**
Now this can be seen as general suggestions that you might want to incorporate in pdt. But until you do so, I'd like to understand how I can go about adding my own definitions to the module. Sharing some examples below:
- early morning
- late evening
- weekend
- mid (for day, week, months, etc)

**5. Couple of likely errors**
Writing some errors that I faced with few examples which should be removed/taken care of.
- 7th of December fails to capture the correct date because of the presence of `of, it's a very common usage that should be included
- 7th next month fails to capture the date again, you can imagine the use case, but not sure how easy is it going to be implement it


All in all I'm thoroughly impressed by your work and would love to contribute further by giving my feedback and my testing results.
Thanks!

issue_title: ConfigFileParse does not handle action='append'
issue_body:
Only the last key/value pair in the file gets stored, eg:

--foo a --foo b --foo c

Will produce options.foo ['a', 'b', 'c']

But a file with:

foo = a
foo = b
foo = c

Will only produce ['c']

issue_body:
`editor.edit('file')` fails when the defined `$EDITOR` contains spaces:

    $ touch /tmp/file
    $ export EDITOR='vim -v'
    $ python -c 'import editor; editor.edit("/tmp/file")' 
    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
      File "editor.py", line 103, in edit
        proc = subprocess.Popen(args, close_fds=True, stdout=stdout)
      File "/System/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.7/lib/python2.7/subprocess.py", line 710, in __init__
        errread, errwrite)
      File "/System/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.7/lib/python2.7/subprocess.py", line 1335, in _execute_child
        raise child_exception

After removing the space, it works:

issue_title: `GetFriends()` `count` and `curosr` don't do anything useful
issue_body: One would imagine that if `count=20`, then no more than 20 items would be returned, but the way it is implemented, 20 items are returned **per page**, and the pages are concatenated to create a result. It will not keep you from getting rate limited at all. I guess since that's the way its documented it makes sense, but I just don't know what the purpose of 'count' is, since the caller doesn't get an updated value for 'cursor', which would allow the caller to do his own paging.

issue_title: [gtk3.py] Blank window, browser embedding fails due to invalid X11 handle
issue_body:
There is no error in console logs. When ran with --debug flag you can see many X11 errors after the call to CreateBrowserSync. Thus it looks like the X11 window handle is invalid. See example log:

```
[0815/213053.597125:INFO:x11.cpp(9)] [Browser process] X error received: type 0,
serial 177, error_code 8, request_code 1, minor_code 0
```

The original reporter didn't provide OS information, but I guess this is only Linux issue.

There was a similar issue in gtk2.py (GTK 2) example reported on the Forum [here](https://groups.google.com/d/topic/cefpython/IKfi_yrIOmY/discussion) and the solution was to show the window before embedding browser, so that window is realized and X11 window handle is available.

Issue originally reported on the Forum:
https://groups.google.com/d/topic/cefpython/Oa1kybcNdh4/discussion

```python
from __future__ import (absolute_import, division, print_function,

        ('maperiod', 15),
    )

        ''' Logging function fot this strategy'''
        dt = dt or self.datas[0].datetime.datetime(tz=EST)
        print('%s, %s' % (dt.isoformat(), txt))
        if len(self.datas) > 1:
            dt = dt or self.datas[0].datetime.datetime(tz=CST)
            print('%s, %s' % (dt.isoformat(), txt))

    def __init__(self):
        self.dataclose = self.datas[0].close

        # To keep track of pending orders and buy price/commission
        self.order = None
        self.buyprice = None
        self.buycomm = None

        # Add a MovingAverageSimple indicator
        self.sma = bt.indicators.MovingAverageSimple(self.datas[0], period=self.params.maperiod)

    def notify_order(self, order):
        if order.status in [order.Submitted, order.Accepted]:
            # Buy/Sell order submitted/accepted to/by broker - Nothing to do
            return

        # Check if an order has been completed
        # Attention: broker could reject order if not enougth cash
        if order.status in [order.Completed, order.Canceled, order.Margin]:
            if order.isbuy():
                self.log(
                    'BUY EXECUTED, Price: %.2f, Cost: %.2f, Comm %.2f' %
                    (order.executed.price,
                     order.executed.value,
                     order.executed.comm))

                self.buyprice = order.executed.price
                self.buycomm = order.executed.comm
            else:  # Sell
                self.log('SELL EXECUTED, Price: %.2f, Cost: %.2f, Comm %.2f' %
                         (order.executed.price,
                          order.executed.value,
                          order.executed.comm))

            self.bar_executed = len(self)

        # Write down: no pending order
        self.order = None

    def notify_trade(self, trade):
        if not trade.isclosed:
            return

        self.log('OPERATION PROFIT, GROSS %.2f, NET %.2f' %
                 (trade.pnl, trade.pnlcomm))

    def next(self):
        # Simply log the closing price of the series from the reference
        self.log('Close, %.2f' % self.dataclose[0])

        # Check if an order is pending ... if yes, we cannot send a 2nd one
        if self.order:
            return

        # Check if we are in the market
        if not self.position:

            # Not yet ... we MIGHT BUY if ...
            if self.dataclose[0] > self.sma[0]:
                # current close less than previous close

                # BUY, BUY, BUY!!! (with default parameters)
                self.log('BUY CREATE, %.2f' % self.dataclose[0])

                # Keep track of the created order to avoid a 2nd order
                self.order = self.buy()

        else:

            # Already in the market ... we might sell
            if self.dataclose[0] < self.sma[0]:
                # SELL, SELL, SELL!!! (with all possible default parameters)
                self.log('SELL CREATE, %.2f' % self.dataclose[0])

                # Keep track of the created order to avoid a 2nd order
                self.order = self.sell()


if __name__ == '__main__':

    staticdata0 = bt.feeds.PandasData(dataname=staticdata0frame)

#    import pdb; pdb.set_trace()

    storekwargs = dict(

        port=7465,
        clientId=90,
        timeoffset=True,
        reconnect=3,
        timeout=3,
        _debug=True
    )

    ibstore = bt.stores.IBStore(**storekwargs)

    broker = ibstore.getbroker()

    cerebro.setbroker(broker)

    # SPY Live Data Timeframe 1 Day
    data0 = ibstore.getdata(dataname='SPY-STK-SMART-USD', backfill_from=staticdata0,
                            timeframe=bt.TimeFrame.Days, compression=1,
                            sessionstart=dt.time(9,30), sessionend=dt.time(16, 0))
    cerebro.resampledata(data0, timeframe=bt.TimeFrame.Days, compression=1)

    # SPY Live data timeframe resampled to 1 Day
    data1 = ibstore.getdata(dataname='ES-201703-GLOBEX-USD',
                            timeframe=bt.TimeFrame.Days, compression=1,
                            sessionstart=dt.time(8,30), sessionend=dt.time(15, 15))
    cerebro.replaydata(data1, timeframe=bt.TimeFrame.Minutes, compression=1)
```

"'help'"
then channel.exec_command("'help'") will not work, the actual worked command should be  channel.exec_command("help"), "help" instead of "'help'".

 def escape_sh(value):

issue_title: Netconf XML requests using pyangbind Class
issue_body:
Hi,

As an experiment, I attempted integrating  ncclient, a python based Netconf client with the pyangbind.
With this on set/get operation of the pyangbind auto-generated class objects, I was able to generate its equivalent edit-config/get Netconf XML messages. 

As you know, the Netconf protocol allows multiple yang objects to be configured with a single edit-config message.The same was taken into consideration. So multiple pyangbind generated yang objects can be set before generating a Netconf edit-config message for them.

My development involved writing a new class called pybindXMLEncoder to generate the XML based Netconf message.The same will be fed to the ncclient APIs to send the required Netconf message.

I also did minor additions to the pybind.py.This was needed to keep track of the list of objects user is setting through set operation before generating the edit-config message.

I feel that this can be useful to the community. if you think this will be of use or if you think you need to know more on the changes I can write to you in mail with details of my changes.

Thanks and regards,
Parameswaran Krishnamurthy

issue_title: datefinder can't find date with Monday, b'cause all "on" substrings are removed, while dateparser could parse it
issue_body:
Example: date_string= 'On Monday, May 9, 2016'
 _find_and_replace() should remove all DateFinder.REPLACEMENT **words**, but in fact it removes all **substrings**, i.e. within words too, 
so,  _find_and_replace() wrongly mutates this into " mday may 9 2016"
i.e. **it wrongly removes "on" from "monday".**
dateparser can't parse " mday may 9 2016"
but it successfully parses  'On Monday, May 9, 2016'
How to correct: force _find_and_replace() function to not touch substrings within words, at least not touch "monday"s

issue_title: DTZ values are incorrect for KPvK and KvKP
issue_body:
From my understanding of DTZ values, they are half-moves until mate, with the sign indicating the player mating with respect to the side to play. However, for a number of fens, `probe_dtz` returns values that differ significantly from stockfish's evaluation (taking into account that stockfish returns moves to mate).

```python
tablebases = chess.syzygy.open_tablebases('...')
fen = '8/8/2P5/7k/3K4/8/8/8 w - - 0 64'
board = chess.Board(fen)
print tablebases.probe_dtz(board) # returns 1 (but stockfish says this is a mate in 10).
```

If I run stockfish, it finds that this position is a mate in 10. This happens for a large number of KPvK boards that I have checked. For other syzygy endgames (KRvK, KQvK, etc), the DTZ values seem correct and agree with stockfish.

Any ideas what could be causing these values to be off?

issue_title: Gaviota tablebases when there are two possible en passant captures
issue_body:
It appears that in when probing Gaviota tablebases there may be an issue when there are two possible en-passant moves on the board. I was processing endgames from a couple of 100,000s of games and I ran into a position (FEN '8/8/8/8/5pPp/8/5K1k/8 b - g3 0 61'  or more precisely into '8/8/8/8/5p1p/8/5KPk/8 w - - 0 61', where g2-g4 is a potential move to consider), where black has two possible e.P. captures.

It appears that the Gaviota tablebase functions do not deal with this correctly, while everything seems fine for the Syzygy tablebases. I am not sure whether this is an issue on the python-chess side or an issue with the actual tablebases. I found a discussion online that discussed an issue that was not quite the same, but indicated that en passant captures might at some point have posed an issue for Gaviota tablebases. Since that was in 2012 it might no longer be an issue and the situation appeared different (it had to do with situations, where the en passant captures where the only moves changing the evaluation and in any case resulted in wrong evaluations).

The error I get is

`Traceback (most recent call last):

  File "<ipython-input-406-5a534f4f1dd5>", line 4, in <module>
    tablebases.probe_dtm(myboard)

  File "C:\ProgramData\Anaconda3\lib\site-packages\chess\gaviota.py", line 1588, in probe_dtm
    dtm = self.egtb_get_dtm(req)

  File "C:\ProgramData\Anaconda3\lib\site-packages\chess\gaviota.py", line 1760, in egtb_get_dtm
    removepiece(ys, yp, j)

  File "C:\ProgramData\Anaconda3\lib\site-packages\chess\gaviota.py", line 1373, in removepiece
    del ys[j]

IndexError: list assignment index out of range`

This is some code to re-produce the issue (I did not have a separate system to test this on):
`import chess.gaviota
tablebases = chess.gaviota.open_tablebases(directory="D:/gaviota/")
tablebases.open_directory(directory="D:/gaviota//3/")
tablebases.open_directory(directory="D:/gaviota/4/")
tablebases.open_directory(directory="D:/gaviota/5/")

# With two en-passant captures this is a problem
myboard = chess.Board('8/8/8/8/5pPp/8/5K1k/8 b - g3 0 61')
myboard.has_legal_en_passant()
tablebases.probe_dtm(myboard)
# Using get_dtm also does not help
tablebases.get_dtm(myboard)

# With one en-passant capture this is not a problem
myboard2 = chess.Board('8/8/8/8/5pP1/8/5K1k/8 b - g3 0 61')
myboard2.has_legal_en_passant()
tablebases.probe_dtm(myboard2)

# Shifted 1 file to the left: With two en-passant captures this is a problem
myboard3 = chess.Board('8/8/8/8/4pPp1/8/4K1k1/8 b - f3 0 61')
myboard3.has_legal_en_passant()
tablebases.probe_dtm(myboard3)

# Shifted 1 file to the left: With one en-passant captures this is not a problem
myboard4 = chess.Board('8/8/8/8/4pP2/8/4K1k1/8 b - f3 0 61')
myboard4.has_legal_en_passant()
tablebases.probe_dtm(myboard4)

# The problem is not that the en passant moves are the only winning moves 
# (here - changed king positions - almost anything wins, still a problem)
myboard5 = chess.Board('K7/8/8/6k1/5pPp/8/8/8 b - g3 0 61')
myboard5.has_legal_en_passant()
tablebases.probe_dtm(myboard5)`


I faced the issue with package version 0.23.5 in Python 3.6 under Windows 10.

issue_title: There seems to be no way to place plugins /outside/ of your main app path.
issue_body:
Basically, I'm trying to tack FlaskBB onto an existing flask app, having them share the overall flask instance.

As such, I have a folder structure like the following:


```
{base dir}
 |--'app'
 |    \- {My extant flask app}
  \-'flaskbb'
      \-'plugins' directory
```

I can force flask_plugins to properly recognize where the plugin dir is by passing in the plugin path as an **absolute** path to `plugin_manager.init_app(app, plugin_folder={itempath})`, but it appears that it then tries to use the literal path as part of an import path (e.g. it fails with `ImportError: No module named 'flaskbb./media/Storage/Scripts/wlnupdates/flaskbb'`).

---

A better solution (IMHO), would be to replace the two parameters currently used (`base_app_folder`, `plugin_folder`) which are concatenated to form the import path with two values, one being the filesystem path to the relevant directory, and the second being the import path to the same directory. 

That would allow things to be arranged however the end-user wants on-disk, and as long as the files in the filesystem path can be imported by the import path, it would work.

issue_body:
Currently it is required for `create=True` to be set when using to `SQLAlchemyDatabaseAdapter` for the first time so that it creates the tables in the database. This should only need to happen once.

- A check should be added so that when the storage adapter is initialized it checks to see if the tables exist.
- If the tables do not exist, then they should be created.
- The `create=True` parameter should be removed.

issue_title: Should extract_params capture `request.json` if applicable?
issue_body:
Hi there,

I've currently implemented OAuth using a GET method, with query string parameters containing the various parameters needed to obtain a token. This works great, so thank you for that!

However, I've been trying to get the same result using a POST method, which I feel should be possible when requesting an OAuth Token (This is using `ResourceOwnerPasswordCredentialsGrant`)

When I try to send my OAuth request as a POST, with the parameters in the JSON body, it doesn't quite work. This is because `extract_params` only looks at `request.form`, where-as the JSON body is in `request.json`

I'm not familiar enough with OAuth to comment on if this is a good idea or not (I figure you guys can!), so I'd be curious to understand why this is not the case today.

At the moment, I can fix this on my own end by adjusting my providers `token_handler` to extract from the JSON body if it feels necessary, but if this is something that makes sense to go into this library .. all the better!

Cheers and thanks :-)

issue_title: Consider using the offset approach used by DRF
issue_body:
In this project at the moment I have not used the `offset` approach which used by Django rest framework and inspired by David Cramer's blog post. The main reason for this is that it doesn't provide "globally unique" cursors. In particular you cannot guarantee that `before=FIRST_ELEMENT_CURSOR` will give the correct data set, as the offset of the first element cannot be calculated. This edge case could possibly be covered by a second query checking for uniqueness on that value, although this would be overkill in most situations.

From a bit of research, Disqus is the only implementation of cursor pagination "in the wild" which makes use of offsets. Both twitter and facebook use id or timestamp based approaches. By encoding the value of *all* fields used in the ordering into the cursor (rather than just the first in DRF's implementation), we don't need offsets and the resulting code is much simpler - in particular when calculating the cursor for every item on the page.

I've always tried to make sure that any paginated set has a truly unique ordering - for most applications either `id` or `created` is sufficient. It also means you can do nice things like `.order_by('-score', '-created')` where score may not be unique enough to guarantee that you don't need large offsets.

Thoughts?

issue_title: concurrent-log-handler not thread/multiprocess safe
issue_body:
Hi

Unfortunately there is a bug in concurrent-log-handler which corrupts logfiles when multiple processes are writing to it at the same time that were started using for example fork or by creating new threads. Here is a demo script that will illustrate the issue:

```
#!/usr/bin/env python3

from logging import getLogger, INFO
from concurrent_log_handler import ConcurrentRotatingFileHandler
import os
from multiprocessing import Pool

llength = 10000


def genlog(a):
    log.info(a*llength)


fname = "mylogfile.log"

log = getLogger()
logfile = os.path.abspath(fname)
rotateHandler = ConcurrentRotatingFileHandler(logfile)
log.addHandler(rotateHandler)
log.setLevel(INFO)

with Pool(300) as p:
        p.map(genlog, ["a"]*50000, 1)

log.removeHandler(rotateHandler)
rotateHandler.close()

with open(fname, "r") as f:
    for idx, l in enumerate(f):
        clength = len(l)
        if clength != llength+1:
            print("Problem in line {}: length is {}".format(idx, clength))
```

Just run it, and it will produce a logfile with some empty lines and some lines with twice the length (tested on Linux) with a very high probability.

The reason for this is that the filehandle for the lockfile is created when the handler is created. Every process that is created thereafter with fork or a similar method will share the same file handle and multiple threads/processes can get the exclusive locks.

From my point of view, the best way to fix this is to create a new file handle somewhere in "emit" or a similar function and close it after the log entry is written.

issue_title: Using cloudant_bluemix function throws an exception
issue_body:
The current `cloudant_bluemix` function doesn't appear to work. It throws an exception because it uses incorrect syntax to create the Cloudant class. 

**Using:**

- cloudant==2.5.0
- Python 2.7.12
- Python 3.6.2

**Steps to reproduce**

The following code illustrates the problem given the `VCAP_SERVICES` environment variable has been set by Bluemix (or manually):
```
import os
from cloudant import cloudant_bluemix
with cloudant_bluemix(os.getenv('VCAP_SERVICES')) as client: 
    print(client.all_dbs())
```

**Expected results**  

A list of databases from your CloudantNoSQL service in Bluemix. e.g.:
```
[u'images', u'items', u'orders', u'status']
```

**Observed results in Python 2.7.12**

When executing the code in Python 2.7.12 the following exception occurs:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "test.py", line 3, in <module>
    with cloudant_bluemix(os.getenv('VCAP_SERVICES')) as client: 
  File "/usr/local/Cellar/python/2.7.13_1/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.7/lib/python2.7/contextlib.py", line 17, in __enter__
    return self.gen.next()
  File "/Users/rofrano/bx-test/venv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/cloudant/__init__.py", line 128, in cloudant_bluemix
    **kwargs
TypeError: __init__() takes exactly 3 arguments (1 given)
```
**Observed results in Python 3.6.2**

When executing the code in Python 3.6.2 the following exception occurs:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "test.py", line 3, in <module>
    with cloudant_bluemix(os.getenv('VCAP_SERVICES')) as client: 
  File "/usr/local/Cellar/python3/3.6.2/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.6/lib/python3.6/contextlib.py", line 81, in __enter__
    return next(self.gen)
  File "/Users/rofrano/github/bx-test/venv/lib/python3.6/site-packages/cloudant/__init__.py", line 128, in cloudant_bluemix
    **kwargs
TypeError: __init__() missing 2 required positional arguments: 'cloudant_user' and 'auth_token'
```
**Offending code on line 128**

Line 128 of `./cloudant/__init__.py` passes all parameters as **kwargs when the first two parameters should be passed as args:
```
    cloudant_session = Cloudant(
        username=service.username,
        password=service.password,
        url=service.url,
        **kwargs
    )
```

**Correct code**

This version of line 128 in `./cloudant/__init__.py` fixes the problem:
```
    cloudant_session = Cloudant(
        service.username,
        service.password,
        **dict(kwargs, url=service.url)
    )
```
It seems to be working fine for me with this change. I can fix it and send a pull request if you want (up to you).

issue_title: Regarding data distribution for multi-GPU implementation
issue_body:
As a test I profiled the training of example MLP network with MNIST dataset on 2 GPUs using nvprof (/usr/local/cuda-9.0/bin/nvprof -o mnist_mlp.nvvp python train_mnist.py --network mlp  --num-epochs 1 --gpus 0,1 --batch-size 200). 

Since the batch size per GPU is 100, there are 70,000 images for training and testing, and each GPU is supposed to work on half of the dataset, I expected to see 350 HtoD MemCpys to send half of the dataset to each of the GPUs. But the profiler result shows there are 700 HtoD MemCpys for each of the two GPUs. 

I have the following questions based on the observation:
1) Does MXNet send the entire dataset to each of the GPUs? If so, why do we need to send the entire dataset to each of the GPUs? 
2) If the entire dataset is sent to both the GPUs, does each of the GPU work on the entire dataset or half of the dataset?

It will be helpful if you can explain the details about the input data distribution for multi-GPUs.

issue_title: Proper seeding of the random number generators for parallel CPU threads and multiple GPU devices
issue_body:
It looks like the random number generators for CPU threads are seeded with consecutive integers. https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/blob/5a480f0237c28231bcf486f39f917b65513ee6a3/src/common/random_generator.h#L92 So if the first generator is seeded with `s`, the `n`-th generator is seeded with `s+n-1`. Is it right? If so, is there a reason? Of course, seeding `std::mt19937` generators with consecutive 32 bit integers does not mean setting their internal states with consecutive integers. However, as far as I know, it is not a good way to seed the generators for parallel use. It could introduce unexpected correlations between random sequences.

The best way may be to divide the entire random number sequence that `mt19937` generates into `N` non-overlapping subsequences (`N` is the number of generators) and seed each generator to produce one of the subsequences. `mt19937` has period of `2^19937`. That is a huge number and, in practice, we can seed the generators to just skip over enough number of random numbers instead of dividing by `N`. C++'s random engines, including `mt19937`, provides `discard(long long n)` method to skip over `n` numbers. We can use this to seed the parallel generators so that the generators safely generate non-overlapping sequence of 2^64 numbers. Or we can call `discard` multiple times for logner sequence. The method is fast enough and usually seeding is not frequent. I think that 2^64 is an enough number for usual use cases. I'm not sure whether the current seeding method provides any guarantee like this.

I think that seeding multiple GPU devices also has the similar problem. cuRAND takes care of the forementioned issue for the threads in a device but we need to care multuple devices. Is the current formula using the magic number and the device id a proven jump function for Philox4_32_10 generator? https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/blob/5a480f0237c28231bcf486f39f917b65513ee6a3/src/resource.cc#L306 If not, I think that we can utilize `skipahead` function which has the same effect of `discard` of c++ standard.

Random number generations could have subtle issues and I'm not aware of the past discussions on the current implementation, so I could miss something. If the above argument looks sound to the community, I'll work on this issue.

issue_title: plone.api.env.adopt_role does not remove security manager context when exception was raised within context manager
issue_body:
In this example you can see that we're making use of adopt_roles 100 times in a single script, the fallback max stack size as per https://github.com/zopefoundation/AccessControl/blob/master/src/AccessControl/ImplPython.py#L535

```    def test_blah(self):
        x = 0
        while x <= 101:
            x = x + 1
            try:
                with plone.api.env.adopt_roles(['Manager']):
                    raise ValueError('wat')
            except ValueError:
                pass
```

This raises the following error:
```Error in test test_blah
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/unittest/case.py", line 329, in run
    testMethod()
  File "tests/test_blah.py", line 1023, in test_blah
    with plone.api.env.adopt_roles(['Manager']):
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/contextlib.py", line 17, in __enter__
    return self.gen.next()
  File "buildout-cache/eggs/plone.api-1.5.1-py2.7.egg/plone/api/env.py", line 118, in _adopt_roles
    sm.addContext(overriding_context)
  File "buildout-cache/eggs/AccessControl-3.0.11-py2.7-linux-x86_64.egg/AccessControl/ImplPython.py", line 604, in addContext
    raise SystemError, 'Excessive recursion'
SystemError: Excessive recursion
```

This can be fixed by amending this section of code (https://github.com/plone/plone.api/blob/master/src/plone/api/env.py#L120) to

```
    try:
        yield
    finally:
        sm.removeContext(overriding_context)
```

I'd like to run the native client on an embedded system (e.g., ARM v8).   

From what I can tell, getting tensorflow and bazel to build from source on ARM is a giant pain -- I was lucky and found some prebuilt wheels that I've been able to use.  

Is it worth the pain to go through building the client?  I need to hand WAV files to the network, in python3. Is there a better option than using the native client?

```        
from neo4j.v1 import GraphDatabase
neo4j = GraphDatabase.driver("bolt://localhost:7687",auth=("neo4j", "belmontjzs1105"))
graph = neo4j
import multiprocessing 

def main(query):
    def run(query, return_):
        with graph.session() as session:
            query_result = session.run(
                query
            )
        return_["answers"] = [rec for rec in query_result]

    manager = multiprocessing.Manager()
    return_ = manager.dict()
    p = multiprocessing.Process(target=run, name="run_query",
                                args=(query, return_))
    p.start()
    p.join(2)  # timeout after 2s
    p.terminate()

query = "MATCH (n) RETURN n LIMIT 1"
# works good
main(query)
# raise error:

main(query)
```
My question is why the second run of the main function would raise the error. It seems related to the 
```p.join(2)```

Any idea about it would be appreciated!

Thank you!

issue_title: hive table_exists should be case insensitive?
issue_body:
Any thoughts on this one?

In https://github.com/spotify/luigi/blob/master/luigi/contrib/hive.py#L141
(possibly here too, but we're not on CDH: https://github.com/spotify/luigi/blob/master/luigi/contrib/hive.py#L192)

Sometimes we have tables that are defined as capitalized, rather than lower case underscored names.  These are easier to read in code if left capitalized, though hive is case insensitive, and will return them as lower case.

E.g. when checking for an existing table `table = 'FooBar'`, stdout will return with `foobar` and the test will fail

This wasn't an issue in older versions, which just checked for string "does not exist" or "Table not found" in stdout.

Would be easy to fix using `return stdout and table.lower() in stdout` or `return stdout and table.lower() in stdout.lower()`

Let me know your thoughts on this.  I can supply a pull request if necessary.

Thanks,
Lin.

issue_title: Dangerus AI_DEG_TO_RAD/AI_RAD_TO_DEG definition
issue_body:
the macro are define as:
```
#define macro(x) (x*delta)
```
so if they are apply to an expression like a+b they multiply only the last value..
this happen in the ColladaLodaer file with the expression:
```
AI_DEG_TO_RAD (std::acos(std::pow(0.1f,1.f/srcLight->mFalloffExponent))+srcLight->mFalloffAngle);
```
I don't know what the formua does maybe is correct as it is since mFalloffAngle is in degree (and is the only value that is converted in radians) and the acos results in radians, and you want the radians as results.. 

any way i think that is better define the macro as 
```
#define macro(x) ((x)*delta)
```

Thanks

issue_title: Collects the exported data into a single array for hazard outputs
issue_body: This makes it easier to manage them in QGIS. In practice, instead of .npz exports, we will have .npy exports for hcurves, hmaps and uhs. Each export will correspond to a single QGIS layer containing all the hazard information without duplication. This is possible now because we do not export anymore the full realizations, only the statistics, i.e. a reasonable about of data. The .npy files will never be too big.

issue_title: Content type not set correctly for S3
issue_body:
This may only be for small files, after seeing #548 
I thought this was worth making into an issue still, I am using 1.7.76, the latest version, and, at least for small files, the content type is set to binary/octet-stream.  I wouldn't be surprised if it was also not working for large files - I have not tested it.

issue_title: Dump dictionary with binary values
issue_body:
Hi guys!
I had to switch from standard json library to simplejson, so I have an issue.

I have dictionaries whose values may be binary image data. Ideal solution is to encode such values using base64 during the dump:

```python
import json

def encoder(obj):
    if isinstance(obj, bytes):
        return base64.b64encode(obj).decode('ascii')
    raise TypeError()

d = {"key": b"\xff"}
json.dumps(d, default=encoder)
```
After the switch, the same code fails with `UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf-8' codec can't decode byte 0xff in position 0: invalid start byte
`
I tried to solve the issue different ways, including subclassing `simplejson.JSONEncoder` but with no luck.

It seems the library tries to decode each value that may be string, including `bytes` into string with specified encoding (`utf-8` by default) and I couldn't switch this off.

As for me, simplejson should call `default` before trying to decode the value itself.

I will highly appreciate any suggestions on this.

Thanks.

P.S. I'm using Python 3.6.3 and simplejson v3.11.1

issue_title: Drop support for ARM launchers
issue_body: I plan to drop support for executable launchers in order to move toward a distlib-based launcher implementation. See [distlib 85](https://bitbucket.org/pypa/distlib/issues/85/add-arm-compatible-launchers) for details.

issue_title: Wheel 0.30.0 no longer supports Python 2.6 - should virtualenv know this?
issue_body:
Hi folks,

I ran across an issue this past week. Wheel version 0.30.0 no longer supports python 2.6. you can see this here 
https://pypi.python.org/pypi/wheel/0.30.0#downloads
What this means is that if you start a virtualenv using the 2.6 binary and forget to use "--no-download", virtualenv will download the latest and greatest version of wheel, 0.30.0 which breaks your env. 

Should virtualenv know this and circumvent the situation by default to requiring --system-site-packages be set?

issue_title: Memory leak during uploading file frequently
issue_body:
We are uploading lots of file frequently in Minio and we saw we have a memory leak. After checking it with memory_profiler we understood that the problem was the Minio connector.
This is the code that we used to profile the memory usage and the result shows that the memory usage  is increasing with time.  
```
import io
import os

from memory_profiler import profile
from minio import Minio

@profile
def _upload_file():
    object_path = ".../6_meg_file.mp3"
    object_name = "test/6_meg_file.mp3"
    bucket_name = "tst"
    file_size = os.stat(object_path).st_size
    with io.open(object_path, mode='rb') as file_data:
        return minio_client.put_object(bucket_name, object_name, file_data, file_size)

minio_client = Minio('<ip>', '<hidden>', '<hidden>', secure=False)
while True:
    result = _upload_file()
    print(result)
```

issue_title: Scrapyd poller stops getting new jobs under load
issue_body:
I've been scratching my head for a bit with a problem and I was wondering if I could solicit some advice.

So we use scrapyd to pop jobs off a queue that implements ISpiderQueue, using the implementation of QueuePoller found here: 

https://github.com/scrapy/scrapyd/blob/master/scrapyd/poller.py

for the most part, this works just fine. When there are lots of jobs, however, the poller appears to stop taking jobs off the queue, causing the queue size to skyrocket. I originally thought this might be due to max_proc / max_proc_per_cpu, but even once the number of jobs has fallen under that threshold the poller doesn't grab new jobs; once the poller stops taking jobs, it won't take any more until scrapy is restarted via supervisor, even if the number of jobs falls under the max_proc limit. 

I've set log level to DEBUG, but nothing jumps out at me: I don't see any exceptions, warnings, or errors. this is the final line of the log when this happens:

``` 2017-04-06T09:39:26+0000 [-] Process finished:  project='default' spider='generic_spider' job='80222d1c1aac11e783660a334fc553ef' pid=7140 log='/var/log/scrapy/default/generic_spider/80222d1c1aac11e783660a334fc553ef.log' items=None ```

I don't think it's a load issue: once scrapy is restarted, the machine catches up on the queue backlog easily, and the CPU / memory on the machine isn't maxed out. 

Here's our implementation of the Poller, it's very similar to the one in the scrapyd repo but maybe I've done something wrong:

https://gist.github.com/hbbtstar/7f874fc08371103ae42b81e609906bea

I'd appreciate any pointers as to where to investigate next, as this leaves me pretty stumped. Thanks!

issue_title: Missing meta options in 0001 migration.
issue_body:
Looks like the 0001 migration is slightly out of sync with the model's meta options. We had to add (in our fork):

```python
        migrations.AlterModelOptions(
            name='htmlpagetranslation',
            options={'default_permissions': (), 'verbose_name': 'SEO Translation', 'managed': True, 'verbose_name_plural': 'SEO Translations'},
        ),
```

to the 0001 migration (stolen from an 0002 migration that `makemigrations` produced inside the fluent_pages package). We updated 0001 instead of adding 0002 to avoid our migrations having dependencies on an 0002 migration that doesn't exist upstream.

You could fix it by running makemigrations and adding an 0002 migration, or by editing 0001 as we have done, since the change doesn't touch the database at all, anyone who has already applied 0001 doesn't actually need to apply any migrations.

issue_title: support arbitrary partition/offset pairs in commit_offset
issue_body:
[SimpleConsumer.commit_offsets](https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/56efe3975ee707f4ec48aa8da81fde9fbbf14dda/pykafka/simpleconsumer.py#L521) only commits the most recently consumed offset for each `OwnedPartition`. It should also support the committing of arbitrary `(partition, offset)` pairs. This would separate the notions of "consumed" and "processed" messages, addressing real-world use cases in which the two concepts are distinct.

Take as an example an Apache Storm topology with a spout that uses a pykafka consumer and sends consumed messages downstream as tuples for further processing. Here, a message is "consumed" at the spout, but it is not considered fully "processed" until it has made it through the entire Storm topology as a tuple. If its offset is committed as soon as it's "consumed", any stoppage of processing before it's "processed" would result in that message being lost. On the other hand, waiting until it's "processed" to commit its offset means that a processing stoppage would result in the tuple being retried next time the consumer was restarted.

This change might expose `PartitionOffsetCommitRequest` as a public API. Whether it does depends mostly on how other similar interfaces in pykafka handle things like this, which I haven't looked deeply into at the moment.

issue_title: Get lint tests working in travis again
Consider making src come from templates
issue_body:
Currently, it's possible to specify [`src`](https://github.com/openshift/openshift-restclient-python/blob/master/openshift/helper/ansible.py#L135-L141) which allows loading a definition from a file. This gets loaded just as a [plain file](https://github.com/ansible/ansible/blob/devel/lib/ansible/module_utils/k8s_common.py#L250-L261).

I think it would be better if this acted more like how some of the Ansible network modules work where `src` gets treated as a template. I'm guessing that the way people will use these Openshift/Kubernetes modules will be very similar to how people use the network modules. For example [vyos_config](https://github.com/ansible/ansible/blob/devel/lib/ansible/modules/network/vyos/vyos_config.py) and [asa_config](https://github.com/ansible/ansible/blob/devel/lib/ansible/modules/network/asa/asa_config.py).

The template handling for these network modules all happens through Ansible's Action Plugins. A few examples can be found [here](https://github.com/ansible/ansible/blob/devel/lib/ansible/plugins/action/net_config.py) and [here](https://github.com/ansible/ansible/blob/devel/lib/ansible/plugins/action/vyos_config.py).

Here are a few [links](https://www.ansible.com/blog/how-to-extend-ansible-through-plugins) to [documentation](http://ndemengel.github.io/2015/01/20/ansible-modules-and-action-plugins/) about Ansbile's Action Plugins.

This would take some changes to the upstream `k8s_common.py` module in Ansible but judging by how the network action plugins are setup, it looks like there should be an action generated for each module here possibly deriving all from a single class as is done with `net_config`. I'm just speculating around the best approach here.

issue_title: Sleeping rapidfire works in diamond workflows.
issue_body:
So I'm running workflows which occasionally have to join then fork back outwards, so as a trivial example..
```
FW1
V
FW2 FW3
V
FW4
V
FW5 FW6
```
If I run two parallel processes of `rlaunch rapidfire`, then one will do `FW1`, and the other will exit as it sees no job immediately able to run.  The problem being I want the other Worker to stay alive so that `FW2` and `FW3` can be done in parallel.

If I instead do two parallel processes of `rlaunch rapidfire --nlaunches infinite` then both workers will stay alive and perform the workflow as I want, but they will live indefinitely, as once all FWs are done, they will get stuck in a loop of sleeping.

I think what is needed is for `rlaunch rapidfire` to behave more like `rlaunch multi`, in which parallel processes will sleep (and stay alive) if they see that there are future jobs that will appear.  The check which enables this is here:

https://github.com/materialsproject/fireworks/blob/master/fireworks/features/multi_launcher.py#L87

So with `nlaunches=0` (ie until 'completion'), a `rapidfire` Worker should stay alive until it sees that there are no more **future jobs possible** for it to complete.

issue_title: Hough Transform: hough_line_peaks error
issue_body:
In developing an image processing script scikit-image(0.13.0-2) that grabs images off a camera and detects lines using the hough_transform with hough_line_peaks, an error appeared related to a lack of lines in the image.  If there is no output from _prominent_peaks() on line 62 of hough_transform.py, the following error occurs:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "theodoliteAlign.py", line 82, in <module>
    a, angle, dist = hough_line_peaks(h,theta,d)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/skimage/transform/hough_transform.py", line 66, in hough_line_peaks
    return (h, angles[a], dists[d])
IndexError: arrays used as indices must be of integer (or boolean) type

This is due to the fact that h, a, d returned from _prominent_peaks are empty matrices and a and d are being used as indexes for the function return.  This happens when grabbing a blank image but previous versions I have used of scikit-image (0.12.3-8) had a different algorithm that didn't fail on blank images.

issue_title: interpolation.pxd:  split to separate .pxd/.pyx ?
issue_body:
Is there a good reason that the shared cython interpolation code is stored within `_shared/interpolation.pxd ` itself rather than having just the declarations in the .pxd and the actual code in a separate interpolation.pyx file?  It seems like splitting into two files would avoid duplicating the C code each time a different .pyx file includes functions from interpolation.pxd.  

For example, denoise_cy.pyx uses only get_pixel3d from that file, but *all* functions in the pxd get included and compiled in denoise_cy.c.

issue_title: <dtml-except> breaks on Python 2
issue_body:
Traceback:
```python
src/Zope/src/App/special_dtml.py:203: in _exec
    result = render_blocks(self._v_blocks, ns)
.../DocumentTemplate-3.0b4-py2.7.egg/DocumentTemplate/_DocumentTemplate.py:148: in render_blocks
    render_blocks_(blocks, rendered, md)
.../DocumentTemplate-3.0b4-py2.7.egg/DocumentTemplate/_DocumentTemplate.py:249: in render_blocks_
    block = block(md)
.../DocumentTemplate-3.0b4-py2.7.egg/DocumentTemplate/DT_Try.py:142: in render
    return self.render_try_except(md)
.../DocumentTemplate-3.0b4-py2.7.egg/DocumentTemplate/DT_Try.py:171: in render_try_except
    traceback.print_exc(100, f)
/usr/lib64/python2.7/traceback.py:233: in print_exc
    print_exception(etype, value, tb, limit, file)
/usr/lib64/python2.7/traceback.py:124: in print_exception
    _print(file, 'Traceback (most recent call last):')
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

file = <_io.StringIO object at 0x7f8ef106e150>, str = 'Traceback (most recent call last):', terminator = '\n'

    def _print(file, str='', terminator='\n'):
>       file.write(str+terminator)
E       TypeError: unicode argument expected, got 'str'
```

Reason: The used `io.StringIO` in `DT_Try.py` expects `unicode` to be written to it, but `traceback.py` from standard lib insists to write `str`.

Possible solution: Use `cStringIO.StringIO` as it was before version 3.0a3 but only on Python 2.

issue_title: Check for None in __doc__ before changing it.
issue_body:
Python code byte-compiled with -OO has doc-strings stripped out.

This creates problems when compiling different packages which changes the doc-strings by doing something like this:
doc += "additional text"
(when the docstring is 'None', this will fail).

mgcv_cubic_splines.py does this and thus creates problems when trying to bytecompile it with the -OO option using cx_freeze/py2exe.

In order to fix this, I would propose to change all the lines  which say:
__doc__ += CubicRegressionSpline.common_doc 
with
__doc__ =__doc__ +CubicRegressionSpline.common_doc if __doc__ else ''

See also:
http://bugs.python.org/issue23189#
http://stackoverflow.com/questions/22299532/unsupported-operand-types-for-nonetype-and-str-winappdbg-error-after-c

issue_title: Add Great3 cuts (at least optionally) to COSMOSCatalog class
issue_body:
The COSMOS galaxy selection for Great3 included a lot of different cuts from the full catalog.  The code where they do this is [here](https://github.com/barnabytprowe/great3-public/blob/master/great3sims/galaxies.py#L375).  We now have a convenient class in GalSim, `COSMOSCatalog` that can select galaxies from the same catalog.  However, it is currently difficult to match the same cuts that were used in Great3.  

There are some cuts made in the `COSMOSCatalog` constructor ([here](https://github.com/GalSim-developers/GalSim/blob/releases/1.3/galsim/scene.py#L176)), but not the same ones, and certainly not nearly as many.  It would be nice if we could at least enable doing all the cuts that Great3 with a few (some optional some default) parameters in either the construction of the `COSMOSCatalog` class or when selecting individual galaxies from it (or a combination of both).

Most of the Great3 cuts are billed as basic goodness checks.  e.g. dmag between real and parametric galaxies, fraction of flux in mask, minimum resolution, etc.  It seems like these could be enabled with a flag `exclude_marginal`maybe?  We already have `exclude_fail` and `exclude_bad` which presumably remove the more extreme bad objects.  I don't have a strong opinion about whether this should default to True, but I'd like to at least make it easy to apply this if desired.  If there are different classes of exclusions here, it might make sense to have more than one kwarg to give people more control if they want.

Some cuts are specific to the Great3 simulation, so probably ought not be the default.  But I think they can be enabled with some parameters like `sn_min`, `sn_max` that the user can provide as appropriate for the simulation they are making.  I'm not sure which others besides the S/N ones are of this type.

It's also possible that some cuts are more appropriately done in the `makeGalaxy` function.  This wasn't how Great3 did it, but it might make sense if `index is None` to allow things like `max_hlr`, `min_hlr`, `max_flux`, `min_flux`, etc. at this point.  The code could get an object at random, and then if it fails any of these checks, pull again.

Pinging @rmandelb @beckermr who have already begun some discussion of this re commit d931338.

issue_title: Implementing the JIRA Software REST API Reference
issue_body:
From what I saw from the code, the GreenHopper implementation is a bit dated and does not use much of the API available on the [JIRA Software REST API Reference](https://docs.atlassian.com/jira-software/REST/cloud/). 

The advantage of this would be that we would have access to a clean json allowing to access agile data, instead of custom fields. For instance the [get issue](https://docs.atlassian.com/jira-software/REST/cloud/#agile/1.0/issue-getIssue) method allows to get information about the epics of the sprints without needing to go through custom fields. Finding information about the sprints of a story can be especially cumbersome as one can read on the "JIRA Software field input formats" section of the [JIRA Cloud REST API Reference](https://docs.atlassian.com/jira/REST/cloud/)  

I came across this problem as I was trying to write code to propagate Stories and Epics from a source project to a target project, where I have three sub teams, which need to have three separate backlogs. I know it is ugly, but can't have the same backlog for a sub-teams doing NodeJS, another one doing iOS and a last one doing Android. Technologies are too different. /) Hopefully I get the time to finish this code and open-source it 😃 .

From what I saw, it might be a good idea to leave `GreenHopperResource` as it is and create an `AgileResource`, from which the following resources could inherit:

- `AgileBoard`
- `AgileEpic`
- `AgileIssue`
- `AgileSprint`

I have started a project on this at https://github.com/esciara/jira/projects/1 . If this is not the best way to move forward with this, please shout. Any constructive feedback welcome.

issue_title: Should corrected radial velocity inherit _FillValue?
issue_body: If a `GateFilter` is used in the `dealias_region_based` or `dealias_unwrap_phase` algorithms, the returned field dictionary will (1) not contain `_FillValue` metadata and (2) the `fill_value` of the data array will be set to `np.ma.default_fill_value`. In my opinion the returned field dictionary should inherit this information from the input velocity field.

issue_title: Support for Strict CbtHardeningLevel
issue_body:
Hi

I am trying to use Ansible with pywinrm to connect to your Windows hosts and have come across an issue with my company's environment. By default all our region's GPO policy's set the CbtHardeningLevel to Strict expect for our development region. Unfortunately python-ntlm3 requests-ntlm and pywinrm do not support this feature making it a no go for me and others that use Windows in our environment.

I've had a very brief play with editing python-ntlm3 to support this going by what I read on a ruby project that has added support for this security feature https://github.com/WinRb/rubyntlm/issues/27 but I am fairly new to Python and this is largely out of my depth. I also don't know if this library is still active and in development.

There is a library by @ianclegg https://github.com/ianclegg/ntlmlib that says supports CBT but the code for this seems to not be fully there (https://github.com/ianclegg/ntlmlib/blob/master/ntlmlib/authentication.py line 181) so I am not sure on the status.

Is there anyone that would be able to help me get this added as I feel like this would be a importance feature for large to enterprise companies to adopt Ansible. I apologise in advanced wiht my lack of skills but I am happy to help out where I can.

Extra Notes:
Channel Binding Tokens Info - https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/dd639324.aspx

issue_title: Cloud Spanner client incorrectly handles NOT_FOUND error returned by create database
issue_body:
While creating a database, is the backend returns NOT_FOUND error, the client assumes it to indicate that instance is not found and thus returns "Instance not found" error message:
https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/spanner/google/cloud/spanner_v1/database.py#L213

This is not always the case as cloud spanner can return NOT_FOUND in other scenarios. For eg in the "CREATE INDEX ... " ddl statement that table that the index is being created on does not exist it would return a NOT_FOUND.  I think the fix would be to just pass the error message returned by the rpc to the user.

This can be reproduced using:
```python
from google.cloud import spanner

"""Creates a database and tables for pattern 1 sample data."""

spanner_client = spanner.Client()

instance = spanner_client.instance("test-instance")



database = instance.database("bitemporal_pattern1", ddl_statements=[

    """CREATE TABLE MyTable (

        Id        String(36) NOT NULL,

        Field1  STRING(36) NOT NULL

) PRIMARY KEY (Id)"""

,

"""CREATE INDEX IDX ON yTable (Field1)"""

])



operation = database.create()



print('Waiting for operation to complete...')

operation.result()
```

issue_title: google-network-daemon / google-ip-forwarding in systemd
issue_body:
The recent refactoring (2018) has caused some issues with knowing when the system has obtained the metadata and parsed the forwarding IPs. When google-ip-forwarding was a standalone daemon, we could wait for it to complete before starting processes that depend on having those IPs.  However, now that it is dynamically spawned, this is no longer possible.  Is there an option to force google-ip-forwarding to run before google-network-daemon returns so we can wait on that?
Right now, we have to put in a time delay which is both problematic and wasteful; we don't want to miss the completion, but we also want the server to start up as quickly as possible. But we have to have something that works if our instance is migrated.

issue_title: VPS搭建SS服务
issue_body:
**本文所述仅用于学习测试，请勿非法用途！**

## 购买VPS

这里以 [vultr](http://www.vultr.com/?ref=7166743) 为例。[vultr](http://www.vultr.com/?ref=7166743) 算是性价比比较高的VPS提供商了，最低 `$2.5/月`，512M/20G SSD。


[vultr](http://www.vultr.com/?ref=7166743) 支持信用卡和 Paypal。建议注册个Paypal，以后还有用到的地方。使用Paypal绑定张银行卡就可以了。vultr 这边选择Paypal充值。需要先充值，可以充值任意金额。这里我充值了`$5`。

1、充值完成后才可以购买服务器。点击 Servers 即可选择服务器。
2、目前只有美国地区服务器才有`$2.5/月`的，其它地区已经告罄。我选了默认的Miami地区；
3、然后选择类型（Server Type）：CentOS7 x64；
4、接下来选择服务器类型（Server Size）：这个看个人了，如果仅仅是搭建个SS服务器，`$2.5/月`就绰绰有余了。
5、后面的选项可以忽略，直接点击`Deploy now`即可。
6、接下来稍等一会服务器就初始化成功了，会告知用户名、密码、IP等信息。是由Xshell登录即可。


## 搭建服务端

搭建服务端非常简单，按照步骤几分钟就搭建好了。需要先使用Xshell登录到服务器。

Debian / Ubuntu:
```
apt-get install python-pip
pip install shadowsocks
```

CentOS:
```
yum install python-setuptools && easy_install pip
pip install shadowsocks
```

根据操作系统类型选择命令复制到shell执行即可。这样还不能使用，需要进行简单配置。

## 配置

创建并编辑配置文件：
```
vi /etc/shadowsocks.json
```

复制下列参数到vi编辑器里：
``` js
{
    "server": "0.0.0.0",
    "port_password": {
        "8381": "foobar1",
        "8382": "foobar2"
    },
    "timeout": 300,
    "method": "aes-256-cfb"
}
```
配置很简单，记得手动修改下。修改后复制到[json.cn](http://json.cn/)验证下格式是否正确。

`port_password`可以配置多账号，每行代表一个账号：`端口:密码`。

前台运行：
```
ssserver -c /etc/shadowsocks.json
```

后台运行：
```
ssserver -c /etc/shadowsocks.json -d start
```

停止后台运行：
```
ssserver -c /etc/shadowsocks.json -d stop
```

查看运行日志：
```
tail -f -n 100 /var/log/shadowsocks.log
```

用 `-h` 查看所有参数。我们直接选择后台运行即可。如果没有报错，ss就运行起来了。

然后要关掉防火墙：
``` shell
systemctl stop firewalld.service #停止firewall
systemctl disable firewalld.service #禁止firewall开机启动
firewall-cmd --state #查看默认防火墙状态（关闭后显示notrunning，开启后显示running）
```
防火墙状态是`not running`说明已经关掉了。也可以不关闭防火墙，自行配置防火墙端口允许访问。

## 下载客户端

### WINDOWS
[SS for Windows](https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-windows/releases/download/4.0.4/Shadowsocks-4.0.4.zip)：SS V4.0.4。官方最新版本。支持SS。

[SSR for Windows](http://160.16.231.71/ssr-4.1.4-win.7z)：SS V4.1.4。支持SS、SSR。

### MACBOOK
[SS for OSX](http://160.16.231.71/ssx-2.6.3.dmg)：SS V2.6.3。官方最新版本。支持SS。

[SSR for OSX](http://160.16.231.71/SSX-NG-R8.dmg)：SS NG-R8。支持SS、SSR。

### 安卓

[SS for Andoird](https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android/releases/download/v4.1.7/shadowsocks-nightly-4.1.7.apk)：SS V4.1.7，无需root。官方最新版本。支持SS。

[SSR for Android](http://160.16.231.71/ssr-3.3.5.apk)：SSR V3.3.5，无需root。支持SS、SSR。

### IPHONE/IPAD
[Shadowrocket($2.99)](http://dwz.cn/3BvX4t)：iOS 9.0 +。支持SS、SSR。

[Wingy(免费)](http://dwz.cn/5vCPvz)：iOS 9.0 +。支持SS、SSR。

## 使用
下载后，打开客户端，配置服务器地址、端口、密码、加密方式(默认aes-256-cfb)，点击确定；然后点击托盘菜单启用代理即可打开google。

![](http://images2015.cnblogs.com/blog/663847/201706/663847-20170604220608118-277305077.png)


## 参考
1、https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks/blob/master/README.md
2、http://www.linuxidc.com/Linux/2015-05/117473.htm

issue_title: cfn2py generates "from troposphere.lambda import Function"
issue_body:
This import results in a syntax error (clash with Python lambda keyword).

With issue #268 the troposphere lambda module was renamed to awslambda to avoid this clash.
Hence, shouldn't cfn2py generate "from troposphere.awslambda import Function"?

issue_title: New engine throws exception when running bundle command
issue_body:
JvmApp.bundles is type BundleField. In v1 engine, BundleField elements are of type BundleProps, but in v2 engine, BundleField elements are of type BundleAdaptor.

When computing fingerprint for BundleField, the helper function makes the assumption that BundleProps is the element type, thus it tries to access fields which only exist in BundleProps but not in BundleAdaptor. This triggers the exception.
https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/targets/jvm_app.py#L170-182

To reproduce, run this command:

## Checklist

 - [x] Provide versions of tools used
 - [x] Described the feature or ways to replicate the issue

```
$ charm version
charm 2.2.0-0ubuntu1~ubuntu16.04.1~ppa2
```

I expected `charm build` to preserve empty directories, I created them on purpose, it's much easier to rsync directory tree than create it from python code.

### What I got

`charm build` silently modified my layer without printing any error, warning or info messages. This resulted in broken charm

issue_title: Build should not pass git URL to daemon
issue_body:
The reason is because it won't work if you pass a private git repository as the build URL.

See docker/compose#2856 for more information, including a simple test-case by @zkf.

Instead, `docker-py` should follow the same steps as `docker`: if the build URL is a git repository, it should clone the repo locally and pass as a tarball to the daemon.

I believe the problem is caused by how `docker-py` performs a build ([here](https://github.com/docker/docker-py/blob/master/docker/api/build.py#L15)) instead of following the same steps as `docker`.

Please let me know if I'm misunderstanding the problem or if there's any other way I can help.

issue_body:
From https://bugs.debian.org/cgi-bin/bugreport.cgi?bug=900777.

When compiling https://github.com/tonsky/FiraCode, the following error occurs:

```
$ fontmake -g FiraCode.glyphs
INFO:fontmake.font_project:Building master UFOs and designspace from Glyphs source
INFO:glyphsLib.parser:Parsing .glyphs file
INFO:glyphsLib:Loading to UFOs
INFO:glyphsLib.util:Writing master_ufo/FiraCode-Regular.ufo
INFO:glyphsLib.util:Writing master_ufo/FiraCode-Bold.ufo
INFO:fontmake.font_project:Building OTFs
INFO:fontmake.font_project:Decomposing glyphs for FiraCode-Regular
INFO:fontmake.font_project:Decomposing glyphs for FiraCode-Bold
INFO:fontmake.font_project:Removing overlaps for FiraCode-Regular
INFO:fontmake.font_project:Removing overlaps for FiraCode-Bold
INFO:fontmake.font_project:Saving OTF for FiraCode-Regular
WARNING:ufo2ft.outlineCompiler:The copyright was normalized for storage in the CFF table and consequently some characters were dropped: 'Copyright Copyright 2015 by Nikita Prokopov'
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/bin/fontmake", line 11, in <module>
    load_entry_point('fontmake==1.4.0', 'console_scripts', 'fontmake')()
...
  File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/fontTools/feaLib/builder.py", line 458, in <listcomp>
    return [l.build() for l in lookups]
  File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/fontTools/feaLib/builder.py", line 1168, in build
    self.setBacktrackCoverage_(prefix, st)
  File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/fontTools/feaLib/builder.py", line 1088, in setBacktrackCoverage_
    coverage = otl.buildCoverage(p, self.glyphMap)
  File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/fontTools/otlLib/builder.py", line 11, in buildCoverage
    self.glyphs = sorted(glyphs, key=glyphMap.__getitem__)
KeyError: ''
```

I dug into this a bit and I believe fontmake is tripping up on this feature source:

```
lookup brace_period {
  ignore sub braceleft \\period' \\period;
  ignore sub \\period \\period' braceright;
  sub braceleft \\period' by \\bullet;
  sub \\period' braceright by \\bullet;
} brace_period;
```

Which is found in the Glyphs source. Is this valid OT feature syntax? Should these double backslashes be ignored?

```
Traceback (most recent call last):

    sql_statements = executor.collect_sql(plan)
  File "/daten/proj/pretix/env/lib/python3.4/site-packages/django/db/migrations/executor.py", line 82, in collect_sql
    migration.apply(project_state, schema_editor, collect_sql=True)
  File "/daten/proj/pretix/env/lib/python3.4/site-packages/django/db/migrations/migration.py", line 108, in apply
    operation.database_forwards(self.app_label, schema_editor, project_state, new_state)
  File "/daten/proj/pretix/env/lib/python3.4/site-packages/django/db/migrations/operations/fields.py", line 139, in database_forwards
    schema_editor.alter_field(from_model, from_field, to_field)
  File "/daten/proj/pretix/env/lib/python3.4/site-packages/django/db/backends/schema.py", line 445, in alter_field
    return self._alter_many_to_many(model, old_field, new_field, strict)
  File "/daten/proj/pretix/env/lib/python3.4/site-packages/django/db/backends/sqlite3/schema.py", line 229, in _alter_many_to_many
    old_field.rel.through._meta.get_field_by_name(old_field.m2m_reverse_field_name())[0],
  File "/daten/proj/pretix/env/lib/python3.4/site-packages/django/utils/functional.py", line 17, in _curried
    return _curried_func(*(args + moreargs), **dict(kwargs, **morekwargs))
  File "/daten/proj/pretix/env/lib/python3.4/site-packages/django/db/models/fields/related.py", line 2221, in _get_m2m_reverse_attr
    return getattr(self, cache_attr)
AttributeError: 'VersionedManyToManyField' object has no attribute '_m2m_reverse_name_cache'
```

The migration looks like

```python
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
from __future__ import unicode_literals

from django.db import models, migrations
import versions.models
import pretixbase.models


class Migration(migrations.Migration):

    dependencies = [
        ('pretixbase', '0002_auto_20150211_2031'),
    ]

    operations = [
        migrations.AlterField(
            model_name='quota',
            name='items',
            field=versions.models.VersionedManyToManyField(verbose_name='Item', to='pretixbase.Item', blank=True, related_name='quotas'),
            preserve_default=True,
        ),
    ]
```
I'm not 100% sure but I'm fairly certain that the migration was created because I explicitely set the ``related_name`` attribute, while it was auto-generated before. Do you think there is an easy way to solve this?

issue_title: Publisher fails to parse HEALTHCHECK_INITIAL_DELAY if the value contains `\r`
issue_body:
When using

```
deis config:push -p staging.env -a my-app
```

if config file being pushed contains Windows style line endings (`\r\n`), then `\r` will be treated as part of the value, which will result in error when trying to parse `HEALTHCHECK_INITIAL_DELAY` from uploaded config:

```
2016/09/03 01:47:43 strconv.ParseInt: parsing "10\r": invalid syntax
```

which results in config push failing with

```
Creating config... Error:
503 SERVICE UNAVAILABLE
detail: aborting, app containers failed to respond to health check
```

Which does not give any idea what was actual problem (bad delay value).

Changing file line endings to UNIX style fixes the problem, but shouldn't all line endings be ignored? This will require developers using Windows to careful work with configs, and in case of the error it is hard to diagnose. The `\r` problem is not obvious as when config is pushed the operation will fail saying that containers failed health-check without any details of actual problem (that Publisher was not able to parse value).

issue_title: Support multiple store-gateways running simultaneously
issue_body:
Both the `registry` and `database` components use the `store-gateway` to store their persistent data. With a large amount of data, we've seen the gateway fall over in some cases (#3479). We implemented multiple gateways in #2929, but there is a `masterLock` mechanism which ensures only one is serving traffic at any one time.

We should investigate supporting multiple gateways running at once. This involves removing the `masterLock` logic, changing the host/port publishing path of the components, and changing `router` to template multiple gateway upstreams.

Some considerations:
- Does it cause problems that all of the containers use the same hostname (`deis-store-gateway`)?
- Is there a possible conflict if the registry and database are talking to different gateways, but modifying the same objects in Ceph via the S3 API?

issue_title: Router ERROR Missing template
issue_body:
Our Deis cluster was provisioned with Deis 1.9.0 on 8/20 on AWS using the stateless-platform.
This morning 8/24 prior to anyone coming into work we started receiving the following on 2 of out 3 of our Deis routers.  The fix was to shutdown the router and restart as a new container.  I also jumped into the containers that were having the error to find that the files were actually present.  This error caused any traffic that was routed to these routers to fail and the router to still be considered healthy as it was responding to pings from the AWS ELB.
